purpose: Freeze the existing Node Loop, plan/task, acceptance/retry and Agent CLI behavior before Go runtime migration. All values are source-relative metadata or sanitized fixture values.

safety.fixture_inputs: sanitized values, a temporary fixture workspace and fake process records only
generator_rules: Never start a real Agent CLI.
Never construct Electron or read desktop runtime configuration.
Only write an explicitly selected artifact below fixtures/migration/p11.
Create and remove an owned temporary fixture workspace for fake process capture.

target_operation_protocol.legacy_compatibility: Node IPC handlers currently return a completed synchronous result or snapshot after the call settles.
target_operation_protocol.migration_rule: Every long-running runtime mutation below changes to HTTP 202 OperationAccepted. Snapshot and SSE converge the visible state; reconnecting queries the same operation and never repeats the mutation.
target_operation_protocol.runtime_owner_rule: An accepted operation fixes its runtime owner. Feature-flag changes never transfer a queued or running operation.
target_operation_protocol.node_fallback_rule: A disabled Go capability may explicitly select the Node runtime, but that fallback uses GoDataClient/application APIs only after Go becomes database owner; it never opens or recovers a Node/sql.js owner.

legacy_error_and_idempotency.input_errors: Node uses existing user-facing errors for missing project/workspace/plan/task/intake, invalid target type/id, invalid batch, unsafe workspace conflict and invalid state. Migration keeps the business classification while REST/MCP map it to stable application error codes without leaking command, workspace or process output.
legacy_error_and_idempotency.loop_start: Repeated start while runtime.running is a no-op and does not create a second timer. A runOnce arriving while busy is a no-op.
legacy_error_and_idempotency.loop_stop: Stopping a non-running runtime is a no-op; stopping active loop work cancels local children before stopped persistence.
legacy_error_and_idempotency.plan_actions: stop rejects a non-running plan; resume and re-execute persist their documented reset each time when their state preconditions hold; recreate only accepts a completed linked plan.
legacy_error_and_idempotency.task_actions: run and run-batches reject project busy; stop with no active child leaves/returns the task pending and records stop_requested.
legacy_error_and_idempotency.acceptance: Repeated accept refreshes accepted_at; repeated unaccept keeps accepted_at null; batch input is deduplicated by target type/id before validation; redo rejects active work.
legacy_error_and_idempotency.intake_retry: Retry rejects closed/completed or already linked intake. It clears the recorded generation failure state but obeys the runOnce busy gate rather than launching a parallel generation.

go_acceptance_retry_actions.ui_adapter: preload acceptance:* and intake:retryGeneratePlan compatibility transport

go_agent_cli_runtime.rest_adapter: internal runtime command only; no raw CLI endpoint
go_agent_cli_runtime.mcp_adapter: none; invoked by the owning runtime action

current_preconditions: workspace path exists in project configuration
workspace is not active for a different project
actions.current_transition: runtime.running false -> true; project_states.running=1 and phase=running; first runOnce is scheduled immediately; one interval replaces any previous interval
event_sequence: plan/task events from runOnce when work exists
actions.terminal_state: running loop waits after a successful cycle; failed cycle records loop.error

actions.current_transition: clear interval; cancel active child operations; persist project_states.running=0 and phase=stopped
event_sequence: task.stopped or operation.stopping for active work
actions.terminal_state: stopped; repeated stop is a local no-op except a fresh snapshot

actions.current_transition: busy gate prevents re-entry; scan pending intake; generate at most one plan; scan plan files; process the earliest runnable plan
actions.terminal_state: idle when manually run, waiting when loop remains running, or stopped if cancellation won the race

current_preconditions: plan or one of its tasks is running
actions.current_transition: cancel matching active operations; pending/running tasks -> blocked; plan -> interrupted
actions.terminal_state: interrupted plan with blocked unfinished tasks

actions.current_transition: blocked tasks -> pending; validation_failed also returns failed tasks to pending; plan -> pending and validation_passed=0
completed tasks -> pending; plan -> pending; validation_passed=0; accepted_at remains unless action is acceptance redo

current_preconditions: source has a linked existing requirement or feedback
actions.current_transition: generate a distinct plan from linked intake; the completed source plan remains unchanged
event_sequence: plan.recreated when a plan id was generated
actions.terminal_state: new plan queued independently or generation failure recorded

current_preconditions: workspace not active for another project
actions.current_transition: draft plan activates to running; task -> running; success writes plan checkbox/progress note before task -> completed and plan progress refresh
actions.terminal_state: completed, pending after failure, cancelled when target deletion wins, or validation result for final acceptance task

current_preconditions: each batch has 2 tasks at most max_parallel_tasks=2
all selected tasks are pending, unique and have disjoint safe scopes
actions.current_transition: each batch is awaited before the next; first failed batch prevents later batches; OpenCode batch is intentionally serialized to preserve its plan session
actions.terminal_state: waiting or idle after batches; failed/cancelled work does not start the next batch

actions.current_transition: active child is killed; non-loop task returns to pending with task.stopped; an active loop stop cascades to the project loop
actions.terminal_state: pending/stopped; late completion must not revive cancelled target

current_preconditions: plan is completed or task is completed/done/passed
actions.current_transition: only accepted_at changes; execution status does not change; repeated accept refreshes timestamp
accepted_at -> null without changing execution status; repeated unaccept remains valid

current_preconditions: neither target nor its plan has active work
actions.current_transition: target accepted_at -> null; plan validation_passed=0 and accepted_at=null; plan/task returns to pending
event_sequence: plan.redo or task.redo carrying sanitized supplement

actions.current_transition: deduplicate preserving order; validate every target before writes; one timestamp is applied to all accepted_at fields
actions.terminal_state: all accepted or no write when any target is invalid

actions.current_transition: deduplicate preserving order; validate every target before writes; one timestamp clears all accepted_at fields
actions.terminal_state: all unaccepted or no write when any target is invalid

current_preconditions: intake exists and is not closed/completed
no linked plan id and no intake-plan link
actions.current_transition: clear plan generation failure count/time/error/log/provider metadata; persist effective generation and Agent CLI selection; request runOnce
event_sequence: runOnce events when busy gate permits
actions.terminal_state: generation follows normal success/failure rules; busy runtime records request but does not re-enter

loop_and_recovery_semantics.project_serialization: Node uses one runtime.busy gate per project. A concurrent runOnce or manual task action is skipped or rejected; no cross-project global worker bound exists in Node.
loop_and_recovery_semantics.workspace_exclusivity: A project cannot start/run a task when another active runtime owns the same normalized workspace.
loop_and_recovery_semantics.startup_recovery: LoopService construction calls resetStoredRuntimeState: active project_states/legacy loop_state running flags are cleared, active phases become stopped, and running plan tasks return to pending. It does not relaunch an old child process.
loop_and_recovery_semantics.late_completion: Every task completion checks plan/task existence; deleted or cancelled targets return cancelled and do not write a final success state.
loop_and_recovery_semantics.error: runOnce failures are recorded as project phase=error plus loop.error; loop:end hook failures become script.hook.error and do not keep runtime.busy true.
loop_and_recovery_semantics.snapshot: Every state-changing public action emits a full snapshot or a throttled runtime patch; active operation tail output is bounded in memory.

plan_and_task_semantics.plan_stop: stop requires an active plan; interruption blocks unfinished pending/running tasks and marks the plan interrupted.

task_retry.timeout: does retry and forces a new session; persisted task and OpenCode plan session identifiers are cleared first

plan_and_task_semantics.plan_file_side_effects: A successful task updates its plan checkbox and AutoPlan progress note before task completion persistence and plan progress refresh. Plan-generation guard restores a plan file changed by an Agent CLI.
plan_and_task_semantics.batch_conflicts: Confirmed batches require unique pending tasks, at least two and at most two tasks per batch, disjoint non-unknown/non-validation scopes, and no test/validation/release style task title. Batches are sequential; task work within an allowed non-OpenCode batch may run together.
plan_and_task_semantics.linked_intake_completion: Completing all linked plans marks nonterminal linked requirements/feedback completed and emits plan.linked_intakes.completed.

acceptance_semantics.orthogonal_state: Manual acceptance only changes accepted_at and never launches validation, scripts, tasks or a loop cycle.
acceptance_semantics.single_item: Accept requires completed plan/task; unaccept permits an existing project-owned target regardless of completed state; both record one event and snapshot.
acceptance_semantics.batch: Batch acceptance/unaccept validates all normalized targets before any write, deduplicates by target type and id preserving order, then emits a per-item event with one final snapshot.
acceptance_semantics.redo: Plan redo delegates to re-execution with accepted_at cleared. Task redo clears task and parent plan acceptance, resets plan validation and returns the selected target to pending. Running targets are rejected.

global.workdir: spawn cwd is the authorized workspace
global.output: stdout/stderr stream into a bounded operation tail and per-operation log; provider-specific final response is copied to last file
global.cancellation: runtime cancellation kills the registered child and archives the operation; process-tree behavior is a P11 runner migration concern

session_fields.rule: Session identifiers are syntactically validated, provider-scoped and represented by short display identifiers in snapshots. Oh-my-pi clears unsupported session fields.

codex.custom_command: configured command is preserved as one command value
codex.session: UUID session id is captured from output; resume failure matching thread/resume/session-missing patterns falls back to new and records requested id plus fallback-new state

claude.custom_command: configured command is preserved as one command value
claude.output_parsing: stream-json activity/result/session parser; final result text becomes last file
claude.session_fallback: session/conversation missing output after a nonzero resume starts a new session and persists fallback-new state
claude.environment_rule: Only nonempty configured overrides are merged over workspace/project environment; their values are never included in snapshots, fixture, events, errors or logs.

opencode.custom_command: configured command is preserved as one command value
opencode.prompt_delivery: positional argument; a Windows cmd multiline or oversized prompt is written to a temporary attachment and a short positional pointer plus -f attachment is used
opencode.output_parsing: stdout becomes last file; title lookup uses session list JSON with max-count 50
opencode.session: plan-level session id and title are used; missing resume session falls back to new; timeout clears plan session id
opencode.plan_agent: Only plan generation attempts to materialize and pass the restricted autoplan-plan agent; task execution does not inject it.

oh_my_pi.custom_command: configured command is preserved as one command value
oh_my_pi.session: stateless; no session fields are retained

agent_cli.future_runner_boundary: Go must keep command and argument-array boundaries for all providers. Node's Windows Codex shell compatibility is documented behavior, not permission for Go to reconstruct a command string or enable shell execution.

inventory.drift_rule: A new or removed runtime IPC handler, process spawn, direct database write or lexical state transition count is a contract drift and fails inventory until this reviewed contract is intentionally updated.
